rationale: The citing study analyzes diffusion/peer influence across board interlock networks and explicitly uses a linear-in-means framework with IV-style identification, while the cited paper is a causal social-interactions/opinion-leader study focused on identification of peer effects under endogeneity. That alignment suggests the 2010 paper could be a methodological or foundational precedent rather than a purely incidental mention, so it should proceed to full relation judgment.
